---

The user wants me to:
1. Read the specs and codebase to understand the project context
2. Improve the landing page by moving the search bar section to be the first thing users see (currently it seems to be after the hero section)
3. Swap the positions of two sections - the "Explore Before You Join" search section should come first, and the current hero with sign-in buttons should come second

Let me first read the relevant specs and then find the landing page code to understand the current structure.Now I understand the structure. The landing page has:
1. **Hero section** - The current hero with SSO buttons (title "The Future of Social Networking")
2. **Live Search section** - The "Discover What Women Are Sharing" section with the Aurora Search Box

The user wants to swap these two sections so that the search section becomes the first thing users see when they land on the page, making it a "door" to immediately engage with the Aurora App ecosystem. Now I'll swap the sections. The strategy is:
1. Move the "LIVE SEARCH" section to be right after the navbar (before the hero)
2. Move the "HERO SECTION" to where the search currently is I've successfully swapped the sections on the landing page. Here's what changed:

**New Layout Order:**

1. **Search Section (First)** - "Explore Before You Join"
   - Now appears immediately after the navbar
   - Users can start interacting with Aurora App search features right away
   - Added the Aurora Shader background and floating orbs for visual impact
   - Changed animations to trigger on page load (`animate`) instead of viewport entry (`whileInView`)
   - Acts as the "door" to engage users with the ecosystem immediately

2. **Hero Section (Second)** - "The Future of Social Networking"  
   - Moved below the search section
   - Contains the sign-up/sign-in CTAs
   - Changed animations to trigger on viewport entry (`whileInView`) since it's now below the fold
   - Removed the scroll indicator since it's no longer the first section
   - Adjusted background to complement the new position (gradient from white to cream)

This reorganization allows:
- **Any visitor** (logged in or not) to immediately engage with the search functionality
- Users to explore the community content, debates, and AI features **before** committing to sign up
- A smoother conversion funnel where users experience value first, then are invited to join

(\r\n        &lt;div className=\"mt-3 px-4 w-full\"&gt;\r\n          &lt;div className=\"p-2 rounded-lg bg-white/50 dark:bg-[var(--color-aurora-violet)]/30 border border-[var(--color-aurora-purple)]/20\"&gt;\r\n            &lt;p className=\"text-sm text-[var(--foreground)] leading-relaxed\"&gt;\r\n              {liveText}\r\n              &lt;span className=\"inline-block w-0.5 h-4 bg-[var(--color-aurora-purple)] animate-pulse ml-0.5 align-middle\" /&gt;\r\n            &lt;/p&gt;\r\n          &lt;/div&gt;\r\n        &lt;/div&gt;\r\n      )}\r\n      &lt;p className=\"mt-2 text-xs text-[var(--muted-foreground)]\"&gt;\r\n        🎤 Speak in any language - auto-detectedDetect user's preferred language for speech recognition\r\nconst getUserLanguage = (): string =&gt; {\r\n  if (typeof window === 'undefined') return 'en-US';\r\n  // Try to get the user's preferred language from the browser\r\n  const browserLang = navigator.language || (navigator as any).userLanguage || 'en-US';\r\n  return browserLang;\r\n};\r\n\r\n// Voice Recorder Component with actual recording and transcription\r\nconst VoiceRecorder: React.FC&lt;{\r\n  isRecording: boolean;\r\n  onStop: (audioBlob: Blob | null) =&gt; void;\r\n  onTranscript?: (text: string) =&gt; void;\r\n  mediaRecorderRef: React.MutableRefObject&lt;MediaRecorder | null&gt;;\r\n  liveText?: string; // Show live transcription\r\n}&gt; = ({ isRecording, onStop, onTranscript, mediaRecorderRef, liveText }) =&gt; {\r\n  const [time, setTime] = useState(0);\r\n  const chunksRef = useRef&lt;Blob[]&gt;([]);\r\n  const recognitionRef = useRef&lt;any&gt;(null);\r\n  const fullTranscriptRef = useRef&lt;string&gt;(\"\");\r\n\r\n  useEffect(() =&gt; {\r\n    if (!isRecording) {\r\n      setTime(0);\r\n      fullTranscriptRef.current = \"\";\r\n      if (recognitionRef.current) {\r\n        recognitionRef.current.stop();\r\n        recognitionRef.current = null;\r\n      }\r\n      return;\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    // Initialize Speech Recognition if available\r\n    const initSpeechRecognition = () =&gt; {\r\n      const { webkitSpeechRecognition, SpeechRecognition } = window as any;\r\n      const SpeechRecognitionConstructor = SpeechRecognition || webkitSpeechRecognition;\r\n\r\n      if (SpeechRecognitionConstructor &amp;&amp; onTranscript) {\r\n        const recognition = new SpeechRecognitionConstructor();\r\n        recognition.continuous = true;\r\n        recognition.interimResults = true;\r\n        // Auto-detect user's language for global support\r\n        recognition.lang = getUserLanguage();\r\n\r\n        recognition.onresult = (event: any) =&gt; {\r\n          let finalTranscript = '';\r\n          let interimTranscript = '';\r\n\r\n          for (let i = 0; i &lt; event.results.length; ++i) {\r\n            if (event.results[i].isFinal) {\r\n              finalTranscript += event.results[i][0].transcript + ' ';\r\n            } else {\r\n              interimTranscript += event.results[i][0].transcript;\r\n            }\r\n          }\r\n\r\n          // Build the complete transcript including interim results\r\n          const completeText = (finalTranscript + interimTranscript).trim();\r\n          if (completeText) {\r\n            onTranscript(completeText);\r\n          }\r\n        };\r\n\r\n        recognition.onerror = (event: any) =&gt; {\r\n          console.warn(\"Speech recognition error\", event.error);\r\n          // Try to restart on recoverable errors\r\n          if (event.error === 'no-speech' || event.error === 'network') {\r\n            try {\r\n              recognition.stop();\r\n              setTimeout(() =&gt; {\r\n                if (isRecording) recognition.start();\r\n              }, 100);\r\n            } catch (e) { /* ignore */ }\r\n          }\r\n        };\r\n\r\n        recognition.onend = () =&gt; {\r\n          // Restart recognition if still recording (handles timeouts)\r\n          if (isRecording &amp;&amp; recognitionRef.current) {\r\n            try {\r\n              recognition.start();\r\n            } catch (e) { /* ignore if already started */ }\r\n          }",

}

The user is pointing out several issues with the Aurora Search Box:

1. **Two record buttons** - There are two microphone buttons visible which is confusing. Looking at the screenshots, I can see there's a microphone button in the mode selection area and another one for the submit action.

2. **Transcription not showing in search bar** - When recording, the transcribed text should appear in the textarea so users can see what's being captured. Currently it seems like the transcription isn't being displayed.

3. **Language support** - The speech recognition should support multiple languages, not just English. The user tested in Spanish and nothing was transcribed.

4. **Voice recording for other modes** - Voice recording seems to only work for "Judge" mode but should work for Web search and Community search too.

Let me look at the aurora-search-box.tsx component to understand the current implementation and fix these issues.I understand the issues you're experiencing. Let me analyze the Aurora Search Box component to fix these problems:

1. **Two record buttons** - Confusing UI, need to unify
2. **Transcription not visible** - Text should appear in the search bar as user speaks
3. **Language support** - Need to detect user's language for speech recognition
4. **Voice recording for all modes** - Should work for Web and Community search, not just Judge Now I understand the issues:

1. **Two record buttons** - Looking at the action buttons area, there's:
   - A dedicated "Record button for live arguments" that only shows in judge mode
   - A main action button that also shows a Mic icon when there's no content

   This creates confusion with duplicate mic buttons. The fix is to unify them - have ONE voice button that works for all modes.

2. **Transcription not showing** - The transcription is being set correctly (`setInput(initialInputRef.current + separator + text)`), but since the textarea is hidden during recording (`{!isRecording && <textarea ...`), users can't see it. We should show the live transcription.

3. **Language support** - The speech recognition is hardcoded to English (`recognition.lang = 'en-US'`). It should detect the user's browser language.

4. **Voice for all modes** - The voice button only shows for judge mode (`{mode === "judge" && !isRecording && !judgeResult && (...`). It should work for all modes.

Let me fix these issues:Now I understand the issues. Let me fix them:

1. **Two mic buttons** - I'll unify them into a single voice button that works for all modes
2. **Transcription visibility** - Show the transcribed text in the textarea while recording
3. **Language support** - Detect user's browser language for speech recognition
4. **Voice for all modes** - Enable voice input for Web and Community search too I've made all the improvements to the Aurora Search Box. Here's a summary of the changes:

## Changes Made:

### 1. **Unified Voice Button** 🎤
- **Before**: Two separate mic buttons (one for Judge mode + one generic action button)
- **After**: Single voice button that works for **all modes** (Judge, Web, Community)
- Clear visual states: Voice button (purple) → Stop button (red pulsing) → Submit button (purple with icon)

### 2. **Live Transcription Visibility** 👁️
- **Before**: Transcription happened in background, users couldn't see what was being captured
- **After**: Live transcription shows in a styled box within the recording UI
- Blinking cursor indicates active transcription
- Text appears in real-time as the user speaks

### 3. **Multi-Language Speech Recognition** 🌍
- **Before**: Hardcoded to `en-US` only
- **After**: Auto-detects user's browser language (`navigator.language`)
- Shows language badge (e.g., "ES", "EN", "FR") during recording
- Message updated to "Speak in any language - auto-detected"
- Added auto-restart on network/timeout errors for better reliability

### 4. **Voice for All Search Modes** 🔍
- **Before**: Voice only worked for Judge mode
- **After**: Voice input works for Web Search and Community Search too
- Same unified experience across all three modes

## Button Logic Now:
| State | Button Shown |
|-------|-------------|
| Empty input, not recording | 🎤 Mic button (start voice) |
| Recording | ⏹️ Stop button (red, pulsing) |
| Has content, not recording | ⬆️ Submit button (Gavel for Judge, Arrow for others) |

This creates a more harmonious, centralized experience where users can use voice in any mode and always see what's being transcribed!
